title: Admin create-user action produces a tamper-evident log entry
preconditions: An admin account exists and is authenticated.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Perform an admin create-user action via the API.
Query the audit log table for the most recent entry.
Verify the entry records the actor ID, action type create-user, target resource, and timestamp.
Verify the entry includes a cryptographic digest or hash field computed over the log record content.
expected_result: A log entry exists with correct metadata fields and a non-empty cryptographic hash value that covers the record content.

title: Admin modify-user action produces a tamper-evident log entry
preconditions: An admin account exists and is authenticated.
A target student account exists.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Perform an admin modify-user action that changes a profile field on the target account.
Query the audit log table for the entry matching this action.
Verify the entry records the actor ID, action type modify-user, target account ID, changed field, and timestamp.
Verify a cryptographic hash field is present and non-empty.
expected_result: A log entry capturing all required metadata fields and a cryptographic hash is present for the modify-user action.

title: Admin deactivate-user action produces a tamper-evident log entry
preconditions: An admin account exists and is authenticated.
An active student account exists.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Perform an admin deactivate-user action on the student account.
Query the audit log table for the matching entry.
Verify the entry records actor ID, action type deactivate-user, target account ID, and timestamp.
Verify the cryptographic hash field is present.
expected_result: A tamper-evident log entry for the deactivate-user action is present with all required metadata and a cryptographic hash.

title: Admin policy-change action produces a tamper-evident log entry
preconditions: An admin account exists and is authenticated.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Perform an admin policy-change action, such as updating the agent retry limit.
Query the audit log table for the matching entry.
Verify the entry records actor ID, action type policy-change, the policy key and new value, and timestamp.
Verify the cryptographic hash field is present and non-empty.
expected_result: A tamper-evident log entry for the policy-change action exists with all required fields and a valid cryptographic hash.

title: Non-admin action does not produce an audit log entry
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Record the current count of rows in the audit log table.
Perform a student-scoped action such as submitting section feedback.
Query the audit log table and compare the row count to the recorded value.
expected_result: The audit log row count is unchanged; no entry is created for the student action.

title: Unmodified audit log passes integrity verification
preconditions: At least three admin actions have been performed and their log entries exist in the database.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Invoke the audit log integrity verification endpoint or function.
Observe the verification result.
expected_result: The integrity verification reports that all log entries are unaltered and the verification result indicates success.

title: Altered audit log entry fails integrity verification
preconditions: At least one admin action log entry exists in the database.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
Direct database write access is available to simulate tampering.
steps: Directly update the action_type field of one audit log row in the database without recomputing its hash.
Invoke the audit log integrity verification endpoint or function.
Observe the verification result.
expected_result: The integrity verification detects the mismatch between the stored hash and the current row content and reports the entry as tampered.

title: Deleted audit log entry is detected by integrity verification
preconditions: A sequence of admin action log entries exists in the database with a chained or sequential integrity structure.
The audit module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
Direct database write access is available to simulate tampering.
steps: Directly delete one audit log row from the middle of the sequence.
Invoke the audit log integrity verification endpoint or function.
Observe the verification result.
expected_result: The integrity verification detects the gap caused by the deleted entry and reports the log as invalid.
